diagrams.title: Basic Logic Gates and Truth Tables
diagrams.description: Shows the three fundamental logic gates (NOT, AND, OR) with their standard IEEE/ANSI symbols and corresponding truth tables. The NOT gate inverts the input, AND produces HIGH only when both inputs are HIGH, OR produces HIGH when at least one input is HIGH.
elements: NOT gate symbol (triangle with circle at output)
AND gate symbol (D-shaped with flat input side)
diagrams.purpose: Introduces the foundational building blocks of digital logic circuits and demonstrates how each gate processes binary inputs to produce outputs

diagrams.title: Derived Logic Gates and Truth Tables
diagrams.description: Shows four derived/composite logic gates (NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR) with their IEEE/ANSI symbols and truth tables. NAND and NOR are universal gates used to create any logic function; XOR outputs HIGH when inputs differ; XNOR outputs HIGH when inputs are equal.
elements: NAND gate symbol (AND with inverted output circle)
NOR gate symbol (OR with inverted output circle)
XOR gate symbol (OR with extra curved line at inputs)
XNOR gate symbol (XOR with inverted output circle)
diagrams.purpose: Explains compound logic gates that are commonly used in digital circuits, highlighting their unique output behaviors for arithmetic and comparison operations
